objects.name: How long do things take, and how spread out is it?

see_instead.when: the third measure adds nothing to the story
rows are entities, not an ordered dimension
the relationship, not time, is the question

alternatives: CH-COR-01 (few enough points to draw raw)

see_instead.when: one pair is already known to matter
deltas chain into a running total
explaining one period's change by driver
the question is what share clears a threshold
one pooled group, outliers by name
too many points to draw raw
few enough values to show raw
categories have no natural band order
the two parts share one whole

alternatives: CH-DST-01 (histogram shows the shape directly, planned)

see_instead.when: the shape of the spread matters
few enough values to show raw

alternatives: CH-DEV-01 (diverging bar, when steps are independent)

see_instead.when: one quantity bridges start to end
the data is a time series

alternatives: CH-DST-02 (dots when the gap is the story)

see_instead.when: the gap between the two measures is the story
labels are short and periods few
values are within one order of magnitude
the share of a whole is the message

alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (when order matters more than area)

see_instead.when: column totals are close to equal

alternatives: CH-FLO-01 (gains and losses, not pure attrition)

see_instead.when: steps can gain as well as lose
the data is a time series
the metric value itself is the story

alternatives: CH-RNK-01 (bar alone when exact lookup is secondary)

see_instead.when: geography adds nothing to the message
rows share one unit and rank matters
values are cumulative progress vs plan
series cross and levels must be read exactly
one metric deserves a full chart
commitment vs plan is the story
series share one unit and scale
